title: poa alpina subsp. badensis
intro: Poa alpina subsp. badensis, also known as Alpine Meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and indigestion. The health benefits of Poa alpina subsp. badensis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas or infusions, and can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
